narrative:
On July 8, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 37.5%, with 972 cases heard out of 2,589 scheduled. This represents a decline in clearance velocity from preceding sessions, continuing a downward trajectory from the 42.5% hearing rate (1,017 of 2,394 scheduled) observed on July 3 and the 42.8% rate on July 1. 

Workloads were highly concentrated, led by Justice S Vishwajith Shetty, who heard 90 of 341 scheduled cases (26.4%). In contrast, Justice M. Nagaprasanna scheduled a total of 212 cases across three lists but heard none. Strong judicial conversion was achieved by Justice Shivashankar Amaranavar, who resolved 53 out of 69 cases (76.8%) and 12 out of 13 cases (92.3%) in separate lists, and Justice V Srishananda, who recorded a 75.9% hearing rate across 83 cases.

Procedural stage data indicates that Orders constituted the largest block with 912 cases scheduled and 413 heard (45.3%), showing minor fluctuation from the 47.0% rate on July 3. Final Hearings struggled to progress, achieving just a 14.7% conversion rate (72 of 489). Among case types, Writ Petitions (WP) led with 1,101 scheduled cases and 411 heard (37.3%). While Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) maintained steady conversion at 44.4% of 358 scheduled matters, Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) recorded a lower rate of 22.9%. Conversely, Regular Second Appeals (RSA) demonstrated efficient clearance, with 75 of 115 cases heard (65.2%).
